user.title: Connect to your Fraimic frame
user.description: Enter your frame's address. Make sure the frame is awake (tap it) — it is unreachable in deep sleep.

data_description.host: mDNS name (fraimic.local) or IP address of the frame.

resolution.title: Which Fraimic model?
resolution.description: Couldn't auto-detect the size of the frame at {host}, so pick the model: Standard Canvas = 13.3" (1600x1200), Large Canvas = 31.5" (2560x1440). Choose Custom only if you know the exact pixel size, and fill in Width + Height.

error.cannot_connect: Could not reach the frame. Is it awake and on the same network?
error.custom_resolution_required: Custom selected — enter both Width and Height.
error.odd_resolution: Height must be divisible by 4 (the frame packs pixels in vertical pairs per half-panel).
error.resolution_too_large: That resolution is too large for the frame to render.

abort.already_configured: This frame is already set up.
abort.cannot_connect: Could not reach the discovered frame.

user.title: New dashboard screen
user.description: A screen renders Home Assistant data natively on the frame. Pick a layout; the next steps choose one widget per slot. Display it with the render_screen service (screen_id), e.g. from a time-pattern automation.

data_description.interval: How long this screen stays up once the playlist scheduler rotates screens. Also a sensible refresh interval for automations today.
data_description.window_after: Optional time-of-day window (with 'Only show before') for the playlist scheduler.

reconfigure.title: Edit dashboard screen
reconfigure.description: Change the screen basics; the following steps re-pick each slot's widget (pre-filled with the current choice).

picture.title: Picture source
picture.description: One full-bleed image shown via the photo pipeline (dithered). Give exactly one source: an online provider (fresh art each rotation), an image URL (e.g. a dashboard-screenshot add-on), or a camera/image entity.

widget.title: Slot {position}: {slot}
widget.description: Choose the widget for the {slot} slot.

error.invalid_screen: One of the screen options is invalid. Check URLs and format fields.
error.picture_source: Give exactly one source: an online provider, an image URL, or a camera/image entity.
error.provider_only_fields: Search query and caption only apply to an online provider source.
error.no_widgets: Every slot was left empty — pick at least one widget.
error.too_many_series: A chart supports at most 3 entities.

init.title: Fraimic options
init.description: These image settings are this frame's defaults — every upload uses them unless the upload_image service call overrides a value.

data_description.scan_interval: How often to poll the frame. The frame is battery-powered; 300 seconds is recommended.
data_description.rotation: Base rotation applied to every uploaded image — use it to match how this frame is mounted (portrait/landscape).
data_description.camera_refresh_interval: How often a camera ‘playing’ on this frame takes a fresh snapshot. Each update is a full ~30 s E-Ink refresh and costs battery — minimum 60. 0 = show once, no auto-refresh.
data_description.mode: auto/error-diffusion for photos, bayer for graphics, none for nearest-colour.
data_description.fit: Fill (crop), Fit (keep aspect — white or black bars), or Stretch.
data_description.contrast: Contrast boost (default 1.4 — pushed hard for the backlight-less panel). 1.0 = no change.
data_description.tone: Filmic S-curve 0-100 (default 25): midtone contrast with shadow/highlight rolloff.
data_description.default_provider: Used by the New artwork button. 'shuffle' picks a random museum.
data_description.nasa_api_key: Free key from api.nasa.gov raises the APOD rate limit (DEMO_KEY works without one).
data_description.unsplash_access_key: Enables the Unsplash photo provider. Free at unsplash.com/developers.
data_description.pexels_api_key: Enables the Pexels photo provider. Free at pexels.com/api.

upload_image.name: Upload image
upload_image.description: Convert an ordinary image to the Fraimic .bin format and display it on the frame.

config_entry_id.name: Frame
config_entry_id.description: Which Fraimic frame to upload to. Only required with multiple frames.

path.name: File path
path.description: Local path to an image file (must be in an allowlisted directory).

url.name: Image URL
url.description: HTTP(S) URL of an image to download and display.

image_entity_id.name: Camera / image entity
image_entity_id.description: A camera or image entity whose current frame should be displayed.

fit.name: Fit mode
fit.description: How to scale the source to the frame: Fill (crop), Fit (keep aspect — white or black bars), or Stretch. Any source size is accepted and scaled.

mode.name: Dither mode
mode.description: How to map to the 6 Spectra colours: auto/error-diffusion for photos, bayer for graphics, none for nearest-colour.

saturation.name: Saturation
saturation.description: Saturation boost before dithering (default 1.15, kept modest; 1.0 = no change).

contrast.name: Contrast
contrast.description: Contrast boost before dithering (default 1.4, pushed hard for the backlight-less panel; 1.0 = no change).

tone.name: Tone curve
tone.description: Filmic S-curve 0-100 (default 25): midtone contrast with shadow/highlight rolloff. 0 disables.

show_online_image.name: Show online image
show_online_image.description: Fetch a fresh image from an online source (museum masterpieces, picture of the day, ...) and display it on the frame.

config_entry_id.name: Frame
config_entry_id.description: Which Fraimic frame to display on. Only required with multiple frames.

provider.name: Source
provider.description: Where to get the image: shuffle (random museum), met, aic, cleveland, wikimedia, bing, apod, or picsum.

query.name: Search query
query.description: Keyword for photo providers that support search. Museums ignore it.

caption.name: Caption
caption.description: Render a small attribution strip (title — artist, source) under the image on the panel.

preview_only.name: Preview only
preview_only.description: Fetch and render only to the Screen preview image entity, without uploading to the frame.

render_screen.name: Render screen
render_screen.description: Render a dashboard screen (widgets in layout slots) from Home Assistant data and display it on the frame.

config_entry_id.name: Frame
config_entry_id.description: Which Fraimic frame to render to. Only required with multiple frames.

screen.name: Screen definition
screen.description: The screen as YAML: layout (full, half_horizontal, half_vertical, quadrant) and one widget per slot. See the integration docs for the widget reference. Alternative: screen_id.

screen_id.name: Stored screen
screen_id.description: The id or name of a screen created in the integration's UI (Add dashboard screen). Alternative to an inline screen definition.

preview_only.name: Preview only
preview_only.description: Render only to the Screen preview image entity without uploading — iterate on a design without spending ~30 s e-ink refreshes and battery.
